name: T-Rex Skull Fragment
description: A fossilized fragment of a Tyrannosaurus rex skull. T-Rex had one of the most powerful bites of any land predator, capable of crushing bone.
funFact: T-Rex had tiny arms but enormous legs — it could run up to 12 mph!

name: Triceratops Horn
description: One of the three distinctive horns from a Triceratops. These horns could grow up to 1 meter long and were used for defense and display.
funFact: Triceratops lived right at the end of the dinosaur era — some individuals may have seen the asteroid impact!

name: Ammonite Shell
description: A spiral-shelled cephalopod that lived in ancient seas. Ammonites are excellent index fossils — their presence tells geologists the exact age of the rock layer.
funFact: Ammonites survived three mass extinctions but were wiped out in the same event that killed the dinosaurs.

name: Woolly Mammoth Tusk
description: A curved tusk from a woolly mammoth. Mammoths used their tusks to sweep snow away from food and to fight rivals.
funFact: Woolly mammoths are so well-preserved in permafrost that scientists have recovered intact DNA — a revival project is underway!

name: Megalodon Tooth
description: A massive tooth from Megalodon, the largest shark to ever live. A single tooth could be over 18 cm long — bigger than your hand.
funFact: Megalodon is estimated to have been 15-20 meters long. A great white shark could fit in its mouth.
